title: Hades
blurb: Greek mythology roguelike. Tight runs, real story, gets out of your way.

title: A Short Hike
blurb: Climb a mountain in an afternoon. Nothing wants anything from you.

title: Stardew Valley
blurb: Inherit a farm, slowly rebuild a life. Easy to load, hard to put down.

title: Outer Wilds
blurb: Knowledge-based exploration. You'll know when to stop, and that's the point.

title: Hollow Knight
blurb: Beautiful, brutal, enormous. The pile fears this one.

title: Celeste
blurb: Hard platformer, surprisingly tender. Beatable in a weekend.

title: Disco Elysium
blurb: RPG that's mostly conversation. You play a wreck of a detective. Read it like a novel.

title: Vampire Survivors
blurb: 20-minute runs, dopamine on tap. Costs less than a coffee.

title: Slay the Spire
blurb: Deckbuilder roguelike. Each run teaches the next. Endlessly replayable.

title: Inscryption
blurb: Card game in a cabin. Goes weird places. Trust it.

title: Slime Rancher 2
blurb: Slimes, plorts, no stakes. Background-music for your brain.

title: Tunic
blurb: Tiny fox, huge world. The manual is part of the puzzle.

title: Cult of the Lamb
blurb: Roguelike + cult management. Cuter and meaner than it looks.

title: Chained Echoes
blurb: Modern JRPG that respects your time. SNES energy, 2020s polish.

title: Pizza Tower
blurb: Cartoon platformer at 200mph. Pure adrenaline, beatable in a week.

title: Subnautica
blurb: Ocean survival. Beautiful and terrifying in equal measure.

title: Return of the Obra Dinn
blurb: Detective puzzle on a dead ship. One weekend, no regrets.

title: Tetris Effect
blurb: Tetris if Tetris was a religious experience. Headphones non-negotiable.

title: Pentiment
blurb: 16th-century murder mystery, illuminated-manuscript art style. Slow burn, deeply rewarding.

title: Balatro
blurb: Poker roguelike that ate everyone's December. One more run. One more.
